coredata.dc:title: NaYF4:Yb, Er with N-GQDs mixture: One-pot hydrothermal synthesis and its luminescent film
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Xiaoxiong
Qin, Xiaohong
Zhang, Wenjun
coredata.dc:description: NaYF4:Yb, Er with nitrogen-doped graphene quantum dots (N-GQDs) mixture was synthesized by a simple one-pot hydrothermal method. The as-obtained mixture shows green color under the irradiation of infrared light (980 nm), and exhibits blue color by means of ultraviolet excitation (365 nm). The luminescence of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs are not affected by each other. And the synthesized mixture directly incorporated into a polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) matrix can obtain a transparent luminescent film. The luminescent film also shows different luminescent colors under different light irradiation (980 nm or 365 nm). This result indicates that it has a potential application value in anti-counterfeiting packaging.
coredata.dcterms:subject: NaYF4:Yb,Er
Nitrogen-doped graphene quantum dots
One-pot hydrothermal
Luminescence
Thin film

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: The X-ray diffraction (XRD) of obtained materials
The scanning electron microscope (SEM) and transmission electron microscope (TEM) of the obtained materials
The X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) of the obtained materials
The luminescence performance of the obtained materials
The luminescent film of the obtained materials

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: This work was supported by the Educational Committee of Hebei Province (No. ZD2017214 ).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The XRD patterns of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs, NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs, and the standard data for hexagonal phase NaYF4(JCPDS NO.28–1192).
The SEM images of NaYF4:Yb, Er (a) and NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs powder (b). TEM image of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs (c).
The EDAX patterns of NaYF4:Yb, Er (a) and NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs (b).
The XPS survey scan of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs (a) and the high-resolution XPS of C1s for NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs.
The Up-conversion emission spectra of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs (a) and the down-conversion excitation and emission spectra of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs (b).

The up-conversion luminescent intensity changed with the different laser power densities of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs (a) and NaYF4:Yb, Er (c); the corresponding laser power dependence of the emission of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs (b) and NaYF4:Yb, Er (d); the absorption spectra of N-GQDs and up-conversion emission spectra of NaYF4:Yb, Er (e).
The optical images of luminescent film containing the NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs one-pot synthesized mixture under natural light (a), 365 nm lamp (b) and a 980 nm laser (c), respectively.

head.ce:title: NaYF4:Yb, Er with N-GQDs mixture: One-pot hydrothermal synthesis and its luminescent film

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Civil Engineering, Hebei University of Engineering, Handan, Hebei, 056038, PR China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Civil Engineering, Hebei University of Engineering, Handan, Hebei, 056038, PR China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemical Engineering, Hebei University of Technology, Tianjin, 300130, PR China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Chemical Engineering, Hebei University of Technology, Tianjin, 300130, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: NaYF4:Yb, Er with nitrogen-doped graphene quantum dots (N-GQDs) mixture was synthesized by a simple one-pot hydrothermal method. The as-obtained mixture shows green color under the irradiation of infrared light (980 nm), and exhibits blue color by means of ultraviolet excitation (365 nm). The luminescence of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs are not affected by each other. And the synthesized mixture directly incorporated into a polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) matrix can obtain a transparent luminescent film. The luminescent film also shows different luminescent colors under different light irradiation (980 nm or 365 nm). This result indicates that it has a potential application value in anti-counterfeiting packaging.

ce:section.ce:para: Various commodities make our life colorful. However, some fake and inferior products are harmful to public economy and human health [1–5]. How to combate counterfeiting becomes an urgent problem. Through many years of exploring and studying, many advanced security technologies have been developed [2,6–9]. Among with these methods, fluorescence anti-counterfeiting with easily distinguishable and convenient is one of the most currently used high-tech anti-counterfeiting strategies. Some fluorescent materials, benefiting from their excellent optical properties, are often fabricated into luminescent security inks and further prepared into luminescent patterns for anti-counterfeiting applications [10–13]. In recent years, lanthanide-doped up-conversion materials, owing to environment-friendly, narrow band emission, longer fluorescence emission lifetime, better chemical stability, tunable fluorescent properties and excellent photostability [14–21], have attracted significant attention in the field of anti-counterfeiting. For instance, Xie et al. first prepared fluorescent ink by dispersing the RGB UCHMs in a solvent of ethanol, water, and glycerol, and then inkjet printed the inks on common paper to produce images which are invisible under ambient conditions, but show red-green-blue luminescence under 980 nm light excitation [22]. Yao et al. printed fluorescent patterns on flexible substrates by using the as-prepared UCMPs inks and the patterns can show visible color at 980 nm [23]. However, the luminescent patterns of these materials are easily copied because they can only emit one color under the excitation of single-wavelength light. This serious disadvantage limit their practical application.

Compared with up-conversion (UC) materials, down conversion (DC) materials have different luminescence mechanism and excitation wavelength. As one of the representatives, graphene quantum dots (GQDs) attracted great attention because of their fascinating properties, such as high solution dispersion, benign biocompatibility, color adjustability, low cost and excellent photostability [24–26]. And a number of researchers reported the applications of GQDs in the field of anti-counterfeiting [27–31]. In order to improve the optical properties of GQDs, nitrogen (N) atoms and other heteroatoms are often be doped into the GQDs which are also used for anti-counterfeiting [32–34]. But, in terms of anti-counterfeiting, the same shortcomings also exist in DC materials. If the two materials can be combined or mixed, their problem may be solved.

Different materials often adopt different synthesis methods, the synthesis of their mixtures or complexes often requires a very long experimental period and complex experimental steps. It is very disadvantageous to their application and development. Therefore, in this work, we try to synthesize the mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs via a simple one-pot hydrothermal technology under the same experimental conditions. Interestingly, the mixture exhibits green color under the excitation of 980 nm and shows blue color under the irradiation of 365 nm. And the luminescence of two materials were not affected by each other. Furthermore, we use the as-prepared mixture as raw material to prepare a luminescent film which can exhibit green and blue light under the excitation of 980 nm and 365 nm, respectively. The luminescent film provides a great application potential in anti-counterfeiting packaging.

Y(NO3)3 .6H2O (99.99%), Yb(NO3)3 .5H2O (99.9%), Er(NO3)3 .6H2O (99.99%), NaF and Ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid disodium salt (EDTA-2Na) were obtained from Wei Kete (Tianjin) Chemical Products Trading Co., Ltd. Citric acid (CA), urea and polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) were purchased from Aladdin (Shanghai, China). All chemicals were of analytical grade and used without further purification. The water mentioned throughout this text all refers to deionized water.

The mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs (NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs) was synthesized by one-pot hydrothermal technology [35,36]. In a typical synthesis, 1.56 mmol Y(NO3)3 .6H2O, 0.4 mmol Yb(NO3)3 .5H2O and 0.04 mmol Er(NO3)3 .6H2O were dissolved into 28 mL water which contained 2 mmol EDTA-2Na. After stirring for 30 min, the 22 mL NaF aqueous solution was added to the above solution under magnetic stirring for 30 min, the molar ratio of lanthanide/EDTA-2Na/NaF = 1:1:12. Then, 1.25 mmol citric acid and 3.75 mmol urea were dissolved into the above solution, and stirred to form a clear solution. Finally, the mixed solution was transferred to a 100 mL autoclave and reacted for 12 h at 180 °C. After the solution was cooled down naturally, the NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs was synthesized. And the part of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs was dried into powder directly for further characterization. Single NaYF4:Yb, Er was prepared by a similar way without adding citric acid and urea.

0.5 mmol citric acid and 1.5 mmol urea were dissolved into 20 mL water, and stirred to form a clear solution. Finally, the solution was transferred into a 50 mL autoclave and reacted for 12 h at 180 °C. After the solution was cooled down naturally, the N-GQDs sample was obtained.

1 g of PVA was dissolved in 20 mL water and stirred to form a clear solution at 90 °C, then 3 mL NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs without any post-treatment was directly putted into the PVA solution and stirred for another 2 h at 90 °C. The luminescent film was prepared by moulding at 50 °C for 6 h. The luminescent film containing the mechanical mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs was prepared by a similar way after NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs were mechanically and evenly mixed.

The X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns were usually taken on Germany Bruker AX by a Cu Kα X-ray radiation. The scanning electron microscopy (SEM) images were obtained using a field-emission scanning electron microscope (Nano SEM 450). Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) were obtained from JEM 2100F (Japan, JEOL) operating at 200 kV accelerating voltage. X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) was carried out on a PHI 5700 ESCA spectrometer utilizing Al HR radiation. The up-conversion emission spectra of the products were analyzed by F96-Pro fluorescence spectrophotometer equipped with an external 980 nm NIR fiber coupled laser system. The downshifting photoluminescence (PL) emission spectra of the obtained materials were measured by a Hitachi F-4600 spectrophotometer.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The X-ray diffraction (XRD) of obtained materials
ce:section.ce:para: As depicted in Fig. 1 , there are the X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of NaYF4:Yb,Er, NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs, N-GQDs and the standard XRD pattern of hexagonal phase NaYF4 (JCPDS NO.28–1192). The peak of NaYF4:Yb, Er is similar with hexagonal phase NaYF4 crystal structure data (JCPDS NO.28–1192). For the N-GQDs, one weak broad peak around 2θ = 27° was observed which consistent with the characteristic diffraction peaks (002) of the N-GQDs [37]. For the XRD of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs, the all peaks are also in accordance with the standard hexagonal phase NaYF4 crystal structure data, which indicate that the presence of N-GQDs does not affect the crystal phase of NaYF4:Yb, Er when NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs are synthesized by one-pot hydrothermal method. No diffraction peaks corresponding to N-GQDs are observed in NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs, may be due to the low concentration of N-GQDs and the weak crystal structure of N-GQDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The scanning electron microscope (SEM) and transmission electron microscope (TEM) of the obtained materials
ce:section.ce:para: The morphology and microstructure of NaYF4:Yb, Er and NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs powder were researched by SEM. Fig. 2 a shows the SEM image of NaYF4:Yb,Er, it can be seen that the NaYF4:Yb, Er sample possesses uniform hexagonal microprisms structure with an average sizes about 7 μm and a top diameter about 2 μm. As shown in Fig. 2b, the structure of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs powder is that a number of nano-sized N-GQDs particles are randomly deposited on the surface of NaYF4:Yb, Er hexagonal microprisms, which preliminarily confirms the mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs is successfully prepared. It is observed that the structure and the size of NaYF4:Yb, Er are not change, suggesting that the presence of N-GQDs did not destroy the structure of NaYF4:Yb,Er. Fig. 2c shows the TEM image of the mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs, we can clearly see that the NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs are both exist. The energy dispersive X-ray analysis (EDAX) of the obtained materials also confirms the presence of N-GQDs. As is depicted in Fig. 3 a, the five elements including Na, Y, F, Yb, Er were detected, which come from NaYF4:Yb,Er. Fig. 3b shows the EDAX diagram of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs, the increase in C and N means the existence of N-GQDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) of the obtained materials
ce:section.ce:para: In order to further identify the preparation of the mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs, the XPS spectrum of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs was recorded, which is shown in Fig. 4 . Fig. 4a shows the full spectrum of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs, it can be observed that six obvious peaks are located at 1071.0, 684.3, 531.4, 399.8, 285.3 and 159.2 eV, which correspond to Na 1s, F 1s, O 1s, N 1s, C 1s and Y 3d, respectively. The high resolution C 1s is shown in Fig. 4b, which is divided into four main peaks are located at 288.15, 286.75, 285.95 and 284.60 eV, ascribing to CO/CN, CO, C–N and C–C/CC, respectively [38]. The results are consistent with the EDAX analysis results, further confirming the synthesis of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The luminescence performance of the obtained materials
ce:section.ce:para: The luminescent properties of the mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs is tested. Fig. 5 a presents the UC emission spectrum of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs under 980 nm laser excitation. The result shows that four major emission peaks of Er3+ are located at 408, 524, 543 and 658 nm, which can be assigned to the transitions of 2H9/2 → 4H15/2, 2H11/2 → 4I15/2, 4S3/2 → 4I15/2 and 4F9/2 → 4I15/2, yielding a bright green color output [39]. For investigating the DC fluorescent properties, the characteristic photoluminescence spectrum excited at 352 nm and excitation spectrum monitored at 440 nm of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs solution is tested and shown in Fig. 5b. It is obviously observed that the material exhibits a clear emission peak at 440 nm under 352 nm excitation, which was assigned to the π-π* electron transition of N-GQDs and leading to a blue light output. The above analysis shows that NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs solution can emits green light at 980 nm illumination due to the existence of NaYF4:Yb, Er and emits blue light at 352 nm illumination because of the presence of N-GQDs.

For researching the UC mechanism of the NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs, the UC emission intensity according to the laser power density of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs is recorded in Fig. 6 a. And it is well known that relation between the up-conversion intensity (IUC) and the infrared pump power (P pump) can be expressed as IUC∝(P pump)n, where n is the number of pump photos involved in the up-conversion process and the n value equal to the slope of the straight line [40]. As shown in Fig. 6b, the n value of the NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs at 543 nm and 658 nm are 1.63 and 1.51, respectively. Moreover, the relationship of NaYF4:Yb, Er and the laser power density is also measured (Fig. 6c and d), which reveals that the value of n has little change compared with the n of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs, but both numbers of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs and NaYF4:Yb, Er are close to the 2, suggesting the energy transition between Yb and Er is a two-photo process [41]. The results show that the adding of N-GQDs does not change the two-photo energy transition of NaYF4:Yb, Er in NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs. As shown in Fig. 6e, the emission spectra of NaYF4:Yb, Er is not included in the absorption regions of N-GQDs. In summary, we infer that the UC and DC luminescent process of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs are independent with each other.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The luminescent film of the obtained materials
ce:section.ce:para: In the field of anti-counterfeiting, the packaging of a product is normally the first line of defense. The NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs material can be used in preparing anti-counterfeit luminescent film. The luminescent film containing the NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs one-pot synthesized mixture was prepared by directly mixing the material with PVA. Fig. 7 a shows the image of luminescent film, which possesses good transparency under natural light. It is can be seen that the luminescent film shows blue light under the 365 nm excitation (Fig. 7b) and green light under the 980 nm excitation (Fig. 7c). The mechanical mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs luminescent PVA thin film also was prepared (Fig. S1, Supplementary material). We can find that the luminescent behavior of the two films is similar. This is mainly because the luminescence of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs does not affect each other. Differently, it takes longer time to prepare the thin film containing the mechanical mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs. NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs are mixed evenly which takes quite some time. Therefore, the luminescent film containing the NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs one-pot synthesized mixture shows greater advantages. And it is simple to prepare, low cost, and has high safety performance, can be used in anti-counterfeiting packaging field.

Above all, the mixture of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs was successfully prepared by a simple one-pot hydrothermal technology. It is obviously that the NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs displays UC green and DC blue color, attributing the existence of NaYF4:Yb, Er and N-GQDs, respectively. And the results infer that the UC and DC luminescent process of NaYF4:Yb,Er/N-GQDs are independent with each other. Finally, the material was used to prepare luminescent film assisted with PVA, which exhibits blue light under 365 nm lamp and green light under 980 nm laser and provides a potential possibility for anti-counterfeiting. We believe that this work will have a certain guiding significance to the design and synthesis of novel anti-counterfeiting materials.
Xiaoxiong Zhang: Conceptualization, Writing - original draft. Xiaohong Qin: Data curation, Investigation, Figure preparation. Wenjun Zhang: Writing - review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was supported by the Educational Committee of Hebei Province (No. ZD2017214).

ce:section.ce:para:
The following is the Supplementary data to this article:

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.optmat.2021.110910.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Kaczmarek AM, Liu Y, Wang C, et al. Lanthanide “Chameleon” Multistage Anti-Counterfeit Materials. Adv. Funct. Mater., 2017, 27(20): 1700258.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A new anti‐counterfeiting feature relying on invisible luminescent full color images printed with lanthanide‐based inks

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Hai X, Feng J, Chen X, et al. Tuning the optical properties of graphene quantum dots for biosensing and bioimaging. J. Mater. Chem. B, 2018, 6(20): 3219-3234.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Graphene quantum dots in analytical science
